name: Reputation Choice
description: A narrative choice that changes faction standing, relationship state, or social trust and can trigger immediate or delayed consequences.
design_notes: Reputation choices need clear commit boundaries and delayed consequence handling. Define reputation_faction_id, reputation point changes, thresholds, branch results, choice history, consequence events, and player-facing explanation. The system should support meaningful ambiguity without making social outcomes feel random or impossible to audit.
design_purpose: Make social, moral, or faction choices affect future dialogue, quests, vendors, allies, or hostility.
Give players a reason to consider relationships and long-term consequences rather than only immediate rewards.
Provide narrative designers with reusable state that can be checked by dialogue, quest, and world systems.
player_fantasy: The player feels their reputation is earned through choices and remembered by people in the world.

parameters.name: reputation_faction_id
parameters.description: Faction, character, or relationship track affected by the choice.

parameters.name: reputation_delta_points
parameters.description: Standing change committed by the selected choice.

parameters.name: reputation_threshold_id
parameters.description: Named threshold used by later content gates and reactions.

parameters.name: consequence_event_id
parameters.description: Immediate or delayed event triggered by the reputation change.

parameters.name: choice_commit_id
parameters.description: Idempotent transaction id for saving and replay prevention.

parameters.name: player_explanation_policy_id
parameters.description: How much reputation impact is shown to the player.

edge_cases: A choice affects multiple factions with opposing reputation changes and delayed events.
The same conversation can be replayed after a save load and must not grant reputation twice.
A player makes a choice offline or in co-op where another player owns the narrative profile.
Localization may soften or intensify wording, changing perceived consequence without data changes.
common_bugs: Reputation points are changed for UI preview and then committed again on confirmation.
Delayed consequence events fire after the faction or quest line has been removed by another branch.
Choice history records node order rather than stable choice_commit_id values.
Threshold checks use greater-than in one system and greater-than-or-equal in another.
balancing_notes: Large reputation swings should be tied to choices with enough context and consequence framing.
Hidden reputation can add role-playing texture, but players need enough feedback to understand social systems.
Delayed consequences are strongest when the later result references the remembered choice clearly.
accessibility_notes: Use clear text explanations and reviewable choice logs for players who need more time to process consequences.
Avoid using only color, tone of voice, or subtle facial animation to communicate reputation changes.

implementation_notes.unity: Commit reputation changes through a narrative transaction that stores choice_commit_id, faction id, delta points, and consequence_event_id. Dialogue, quest, and vendor systems should read thresholds from the same reputation service.
implementation_notes.godot: Use a narrative-state autoload to apply reputation deltas and emit consequence events exactly once. Dialogue Resources should reference stable faction and threshold ids, not localized display labels.
implementation_notes.unreal: Store faction reputation in save data or a replicated narrative component. Gameplay Tags or Data Assets can define thresholds, while dialogue and quest systems consume the same committed reputation state.
implementation_notes.web: Keep reputation tracks and choice commits in profile JSON. Apply choices with pure transaction functions so browser refreshes, retries, or offline sync cannot duplicate consequences.

relationships.reason: Reputation choices change faction values that later gate dialogue, shop, or quest options.
Reputation-affecting choices need explicit commit UI and consequence text when appropriate.
Faction reputation can provide readable discounts, markups, or vendor access changes.

example_games.title: Fallout: New Vegas
example_games.note: Uses faction reputation to alter reactions and access to content.

example_games.title: Dragon Age: Origins
example_games.note: Uses companion approval and dialogue choices to alter relationships.
